# Name: Maxwell Braseth 
# Date: 07/21/2026
# Course : DS 6021 - Machine Learning I - Introduction to Predictive Modeling
# Module 9 Homework

```{r}
# Name: Maxwell Braseth 
# Date: 07/21/2026
# Course : DS 6021 - Machine Learning I - Introduction to Predictive Modeling
# Module 9 Homework

# REQUIRED FOR QUARTO PDF — prevents LaTeX # errors
options(prType = "plain")
options(grType = "none")

library(NHANES)
library(rms)
library(dplyr)
library(glmnet)
library(knitr)
library(Hmisc)

# Task 1. Fit the full/partial/no-sharing ladder and report CV-MSE

data(NHANES)

nhanes_new_data <- NHANES |>
  select(Testosterone, HealthGen, BMI, Age) |>
  na.omit() |>
  as.data.frame()

nhanes_new_data$HealthGen <- factor(nhanes_new_data$HealthGen)
nhanes_new_data$HealthGen <- droplevels(nhanes_new_data$HealthGen)

new_dd_nhanes <- datadist(nhanes_new_data)
options(datadist = "new_dd_nhanes")

cat("Complete number of cases:", nrow(nhanes_new_data), "\n")

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Fit first model to extract knots safely
# ------------------------------------------------------------
fit_model_fully_shared <- ols(Testosterone ~ rcs(BMI, 5),
                              data = nhanes_new_data, x = TRUE, y = TRUE)

# CORRECT KNOT EXTRACTION (works in all versions)
bmi_knots <- fit_model_fully_shared$design$BMI$parms

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Fit ladder models using extracted knots
# ------------------------------------------------------------

fit_model_partial_shared <- ols(Testosterone ~ rcs(BMI, bmi_knots) + HealthGen,
                                data = nhanes_new_data, x = TRUE, y = TRUE)

fit_model_full_interaction <- ols(Testosterone ~ rcs(BMI, bmi_knots) * HealthGen,
                                  data = nhanes_new_data, x = TRUE, y = TRUE)

# Fully separate
fitted_model_healthGen_Excellent <- ols(Testosterone ~ rcs(BMI, bmi_knots),
                                        data = nhanes_new_data |> filter(HealthGen == "Excellent"))
fitted_model_healthGen_Vgood <- ols(Testosterone ~ rcs(BMI, bmi_knots),
                                    data = nhanes_new_data |> filter(HealthGen == "Vgood"))
fitted_model_healthGen_Good <- ols(Testosterone ~ rcs(BMI, bmi_knots),
                                   data = nhanes_new_data |> filter(HealthGen == "Good"))
fitted_model_healthGen_Poor <- ols(Testosterone ~ rcs(BMI, bmi_knots),
                                   data = nhanes_new_data |> filter(HealthGen == "Poor"))
fitted_model_healthGen_Fair <- ols(Testosterone ~ rcs(BMI, bmi_knots),
                                   data = nhanes_new_data |> filter(HealthGen == "Fair"))

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# CV-MSE LADDER
# ------------------------------------------------------------

k <- 5
folds <- sample(rep(1:k, length.out = nrow(nhanes_new_data)))

cv_mse <- function(type) {
  mse <- numeric(k)
  for (i in 1:k) {
    train <- nhanes_new_data[folds != i, ]
    test  <- nhanes_new_data[folds == i, ]
    
    if (type == "shared") {
      f <- ols(Testosterone ~ rcs(BMI, 5), data = train)
      p <- predict(f, test)
      
    } else if (type == "offset") {
      f <- ols(Testosterone ~ rcs(BMI, 5) + HealthGen, data = train)
      p <- predict(f, test)
      
    } else if (type == "interaction") {
      f <- ols(Testosterone ~ rcs(BMI, 5) * HealthGen, data = train)
      p <- predict(f, test)
      
    } else {  # fully separate
      p <- rep(NA_real_, nrow(test))
      for (g in levels(train$HealthGen)) {
        tr  <- train[train$HealthGen == g, ]
        idx <- which(test$HealthGen == g)
        if (nrow(tr) >= 20 && length(idx) > 0) {
          p[idx] <- predict(ols(Testosterone ~ rcs(BMI, 5), data = tr), test[idx, ])
        } else {
          p[idx] <- mean(tr$Testosterone, na.rm = TRUE)
        }
      }
    }
    mse[i] <- mean((test$Testosterone - p)^2, na.rm = TRUE)
  }
  mean(mse)
}

results <- data.frame(
  Model  = c("1. Fully shared",
             "2. Shared-shape offset",
             "3. Full interaction",
             "4. Fully separate"),
  CV_MSE = c(cv_mse("shared"),
             cv_mse("offset"),
             cv_mse("interaction"),
             cv_mse("separate"))
)

kable(results, digits = 1,
      caption = "5-fold Cross-Validated MSE – Information Sharing Ladder")

best <- results$Model[which.min(results$CV_MSE)]
cat("\n====================================================\n")
cat("Data support (lowest CV-MSE):", best, "\n")
cat("====================================================\n")



# Task 2. Fit a group-specific response surface: two continuous predictors, additive-in-group vs. interactive-in-group (rcs(x1,4)*rcs(x2,4) + group vs. rcs(x1,4)*rcs(x2,4)*group). Compare CV-MSE between the two, and produce at least one plotly surface per group for the interactive model.

# Task 3. Reparameterize the group predictor with sum-to-zero contrasts (contr.sum) as in this chapter, build the global/deviation penalty.factor vector via attr(X, "assign"), and fit both ridge and lasso. Report, at the cross-validated lambda λ, which groups’ curves get pulled essentially back to the shared curve and which remain distinct.

# Task 4. One-paragraph conclusion: does your dataset support a fully shared, fully separate, or regularized in-between model for the grouping variable you chose? Justify using both predictive evidence (CV-MSE across the ladder and the regularized fit) and interpretive evidence (which groups’ coefficients/curves shrank the most, and whether that tracked group size, distinctiveness of the group’s curve, or something else — be honest if the pattern doesn’t look clean).

# Task 5. In the notes above, the R command : "contrasts(nhanes_reg$Race1) <- contr.sum(nlevels(nhanes_reg$Race1))" implemented a reparameterization of the race indicator variables. Derive the transformation matrix R that takes the standard parameterization to the parameterization implemented by conctr.sum. In other words, what is R such that Z = X * R                                                                           where X and Z are as follows: "X <- model.matrix(~Race1, data = nhanes)" and "Z <- model.matrix(~Race1, data = nhanes_reg)"

```
